name: MATLAB
description: The industry-standard numerical computing environment for aerospace engineering. Used for trajectory analysis, control system design, signal processing, and mathematical modelling across every major space programme.
whyUseIt: MATLAB's Aerospace Toolbox and Control System Toolbox provide purpose-built functions for orbital mechanics, attitude dynamics, and GNC design. Most aerospace engineers learn MATLAB first and use it throughout their careers.

vocabulary.definition: MATLAB's graphical simulation environment for modelling dynamic systems
MATLAB toolbox with functions for coordinate transforms, atmosphere models, and orbital mechanics

beginnerMistakes: Using MATLAB when Python would be simpler and free
Writing procedural code instead of vectorised operations
